name: From Molina de Aragón to Embid Castle — circular via ZEC Natural Monument Sierra de Caldereros
name_original: De Molina de Aragón al Castillo de Embid — circular por ZEC Monumento Natural Sierra de Caldereros
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Molina De Aragón traverse a high-altitude region characterized by unpopulated expanses and diverse terrain. The area features extensive river gorges carved by the Tajo and Gallo rivers, alongside savin and pine forests. Cyclists encounter unique geological formations, including rocky silhouettes and high plains known as parameras, offering varied landscapes and significant elevation changes. This part of the Molina de Aragón-Alto Tajo Geopark provides a tranquil environment for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Molina De Aragón The most popular road cycling route is Valle de la Hoz and ascent to Cuevas Labradas — circular from Molina de Aragón, a 37.4 miles (60.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through river valleys. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Río Bullones from Molina de Aragón — circular via the ZEC Montes de Picaza, a difficult 61.1 miles (98.3 km) path. This route explores the Río Bullones area and the Montes de Picaza, offering challenging climbs and scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the From Molina de Aragón to Embid Castle — circular via ZEC Natural Monument Sierra de Caldereros, a 49.6 miles (79.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Caldereros Natural Monument, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Molina De Aragón is defined by dramatic river gorges, dense forests, and high, unpopulated plains. The network offers options for moderate and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Molina De Aragón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 90 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Molina De Aragón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect on road cycling routes in Molina de Aragón?
A: Road cycling in Molina de Aragón offers a diverse experience, traversing a high-altitude region with striking natural landscapes. You'll encounter extensive river gorges carved by the Tajo and Gallo rivers, savin and pine forests, and unique geological formations like rocky silhouettes and high plains known as parameras. The routes often feature significant elevation changes, providing both scenic beauty and a good challenge.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Molina de Aragón?
A: Spring and autumn are highly recommended for road cycling in Molina de Aragón. During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable, avoiding the extremes of summer heat or winter cold. This allows for a more enjoyable experience as you explore the region's varied terrain.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced road cyclists in Molina de Aragón?
A: Yes, Molina de Aragón offers several challenging routes for experienced road cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017683404/rio-bullones-from-molina-de-aragon-circular-via-the-zec-montes-de-picaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Bullones from Molina de Aragón — circular via the ZEC Montes de Picaza&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 98.3 km path with over 1400m of elevation gain, exploring the Montes de Picaza. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Narrow pass near the Tajo River – San Pedro Bridge loop from Corduente (núcleo)&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 98.5 km with similar elevation.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is part of the Molina de Aragón-Alto Tajo Geopark and encompasses the Alto Tajo Natural Park, known for its river gorges. You can encounter unique geological formations like the 'enchanted rock cities' of Chequilla. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2551153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Hoz&lt;/a&gt;, nestled in limestone walls, and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4322842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7657727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Puerta Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6621844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallo River Ford&lt;/a&gt; also offer scenic stops.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest to visit while cycling?
A: Absolutely. Beyond its natural beauty, Molina de Aragón boasts cultural and historical points of interest. The prominent Molina de Aragón Castle adds historical charm. Many routes pass through picturesque medieval towns with Romanesque-Gothic churches, especially along sections of the historic Camino del Cid. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2551153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen de la Hoz&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant historical and religious site.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Molina de Aragón?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Molina de Aragón are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018270595/valle-de-la-hoz-and-ascent-to-cuevas-labradas-circular-from-molina-de-aragon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de la Hoz and ascent to Cuevas Labradas — circular from Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017717448/from-molina-de-aragon-to-embid-castle-circular-via-zec-natural-monument-sierra-de-caldereros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Molina de Aragón to Embid Castle — circular via ZEC Natural Monument Sierra de Caldereros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Molina de Aragón?
A: As Molina de Aragón is a less populated area, parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as common starting points for routes. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot, as many tours will indicate suitable parking areas near their start. The low traffic in the region also makes roadside parking in designated areas a viable option.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Molina de Aragón?
A: While Molina de Aragón is known for its challenging terrain, there are moderate routes that could be suitable for families with older, more experienced children. The region's routes generally feature significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018270595/valle-de-la-hoz-and-ascent-to-cuevas-labradas-circular-from-molina-de-aragon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de la Hoz and ascent to Cuevas Labradas — circular from Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 60.2 km route with substantial climbing, which might be manageable for fit families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes in Molina de Aragón?
A: While the region is generally dog-friendly in its natural spaces, road cycling with dogs can be challenging due to the distances, speeds, and potential for traffic, even on quiet roads. If you plan to bring your dog, ensure they are well-trained, accustomed to running alongside a bike, and that you have a plan for their hydration and rest. Always prioritize their safety and comfort on paved surfaces.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: Yes, as you cycle through the medieval towns and villages of Molina de Aragón, you will find opportunities for refreshments and accommodation. These smaller settlements often have local cafes, restaurants, and guesthouses where you can refuel and rest. It's a good idea to plan your stops in advance, especially on longer routes, as services might be spread out due to the region's unpopulated nature.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Molina de Aragón?
A: The road cycling routes in Molina de Aragón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the tranquil environment, the stunning natural landscapes, and the low traffic, which allows for an immersive and safe riding experience through dramatic river gorges and dense forests.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Molina de Aragón?
A: There are over 10 dedicated road cycling routes around Molina de Aragón, offering a variety of experiences. These routes range from moderate to difficult, catering to different fitness levels and preferences for elevation gain and distance.
tips: Beautiful and picturesque town in the interior of Guadalajara, very close to the Sierra de Caldereros Park and surrounded by a wonderful landscape typical of this area, between pastures and hills. The Romanesque hermitage of Nuestra Señora de la Carrasca also stands out, which can be visited.

The church is the main element of the town of Campillo de Dueñas where it is well known for its past and especially for the tower that is located near the center, the Castillo de Zafra. This castle located in the south, has been present in films and television series.

The Embid castle is another fortified example of the power of the Molina lordship, protecting the trade routes to the north of the province of Guadalajara, bordering Teruel. A magnificent structure worth seeing.
